name: Texas Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Program
provider.parentOrganization: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

address.description: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation, P.O. Box 12157, Austin, TX 78711-2157

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

requiredDocuments.name: Clinical Fellowship verification
requiredDocuments.description: CF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.

requiredDocuments.name: Fingerprint submission
requiredDocuments.description: Completed via the TDLR FAST Pass system.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Texas SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $80, paid to TDLR. You must also pay approximately $37 for the fingerprint-based background check, bringing the total to about $117.

mainEntity.name: Does Texas require the Praxis exam for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. All initial applicants must pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331) with a score of 162 or higher.

mainEntity.name: Can I practice SLP via telepractice in Texas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Texas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. You must hold a valid Texas SLP license to provide services to patients located in Texas, regardless of where you are physically located.

mainEntity.name: Does Texas have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Texas participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Texas without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Texas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
